programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 6
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and eggbeater kick. Learn stride entries, surface dives, breaststroke and interval training.  Work towards a continuous swim of 150 meters.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 5.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 6
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and eggbeater kick. Learn stride entries, surface dives, breaststroke and interval training.  Work towards a continuous swim of 150 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 5.This is a combined Ultra 6/7.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 7
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives, eggbeater kick, front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke. Learn shallow dives into deep water and treading water while fully clothed. Work towards a continuous swim of 200 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 6.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 7
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives, eggbeater kick, front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke. Learn shallow dives into deep water and treading water while fully clothed. Work towards a continuous swim of 200 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 6.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 7
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives, eggbeater kick, front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke. Learn shallow dives into deep water and treading water while fully clothed. Work towards a continuous swim of 200 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 6.This is a combined Ultra 6/7.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 7 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives, eggbeater kick, front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke. Learn shallow dives into deep water and treading water while fully clothed. Work towards a continuous swim of 200 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 6.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 8
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop surface dives with underwater swimming, interval training, object support and all strokes. Work towards a continuous swim of 300 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 9
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop non-contact rescues and stroke improvement. Learn stride jump. Work towards a continuous swim of 400 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 8.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 9 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side gliding and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 9 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop non-contact rescues and stroke improvement. Learn stride jump. Work towards a continuous swim of 400 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 8.

programs.name: Adult Swim 1
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres.
programs.location: Wexford Collegiate School for the Arts

programs.name: Philpott Tennis
programs.description: This instructional tennis program is available for free, thanks to the generous support of Philpott Tennis and its donors. The program is run by qualified instructors hired and supervised by Philpott Tennis and all equipment is provided.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 1: Penguin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.
programs.location: Wexford Collegiate School for the Arts

programs.name: Preschool Swim 1: Penguin
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 2: Stingray
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop entering and exiting the pool, bobbing, and floating without assistance. Learn front swim. Work towards a continuous swim of three metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 1 or Guardian Swim 3.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 2: Stingray
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop entering and exiting the pool, bobbing, and floating without assistance. Learn front swim. Work towards a continuous swim of three metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 1 or Guardian Swim 2.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 3: Otter
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop front and back swim. Learn side glides, sculling and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Preschool Swim 2: Stingray or Guardian Swim 3

programs.name: Ultra Swim 1
programs.description: Make a splash with lessons to improve your swim skills. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively and float with assistance.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 2
programs.description: Jump into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front swim, back kicks and glides. Learn how to stay afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 1 or Preschool Swim  3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 2 - Small Group
programs.description: Jump into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front swim, back kicks and glides. Learn how to stay afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 1 or Preschool Swim  3.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Toronto Women's Softball: House League B Division
programs.description: The B Division is recommended for recreational players with some playing experience. Participants may register individually or as part of a team. All players must email marcel.vieira@toronto.ca with a brief summary of their playing experience.Softball games may take place at either of the two diamonds. Full schedules will be shared with each team prior to the start of the season.

programs.name: Toronto Women's Softball: House League Ball Busters
programs.description: The B Division is recommended for recreational players with some playing experience. This barcode is for existing players who are part of this team. Players can sign up as part of a team or individually and must email marcel.vieira@toronto.ca with your playing experience.

programs.name: Toronto Women's Softball: House League B Division
programs.description: The B Division is recommended for recreational players with some playing experience. Participants may register individually or as part of a team. All players must email marcel.vieira@toronto.ca with a brief summary of their playing experience.Softball games may take place at either of the two diamonds. Full schedules will be shared with each team prior to the start of the season.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Toronto Women's Softball: House League B Division
programs.description: The B Division is recommended for recreational players with some playing experience. Participants may register individually or as part of a team. All players must email marcel.vieira@toronto.ca with a brief summary of their playing experience.Softball games may take place at either of the two diamonds. Full schedules will be shared with each team prior to the start of the season.

programs.name: Toronto Women's Softball: House League Ball Busters
programs.description: The B Division is recommended for recreational players with some playing experience. This barcode is for existing players who are part of this team. New players to the Toronto Women's Softball League use barcode 140493  to register. Players can sign up as part of a team or individually and must email marcel.vieira@toronto.ca with your playing experience.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Workshop: Crafty Creations
programs.description: Join us for a summer craft

programs.name: Workshop: Watercolour - Wet-on-Wet
programs.description: Explore the fluid beauty of watercolour in this hands-on program focused on the wet-on-wet technique! Participants will learn how to paint on damp paper to create soft blends, dreamy textures, and organic shapes that flow effortlessly across the page. Through guided practice and creative exploration, you’ll experiment with layering colours, controlling water, and embracing the natural movement of paint. Perfect for beginners and experienced artists alike, this program offers a relaxing and expressive environment to build confidence and create stunning, atmospheric artwork.

programs.name: Workshop: Watercolour - Wet-On-Wet
programs.description: Explore the fluid beauty of watercolour in this hands-on program focused on the wet-on-wet technique! Participants will learn how to paint on damp paper to create soft blends, dreamy textures, and organic shapes that flow effortlessly across the page. Through guided practice and creative exploration, you’ll experiment with layering colours, controlling water, and embracing the natural movement of paint. Perfect for beginners and experienced artists alike, this program offers a relaxing and expressive environment to build confidence and create stunning, atmospheric artwork.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Boot Camp
programs.description: Bringing the principles of high-intensity interval training to the pool, this fast-paced activity uses a variety of exercises and is delivered in both shallow and deep water.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Workshop: Crafty Creations
programs.description: Join us for a summer craft

programs.name: Workshop: Watercolour - Wet-on-Wet
programs.description: Explore the fluid beauty of watercolour in this hands-on program focused on the wet-on-wet technique! Participants will learn how to paint on damp paper to create soft blends, dreamy textures, and organic shapes that flow effortlessly across the page. Through guided practice and creative exploration, you’ll experiment with layering colours, controlling water, and embracing the natural movement of paint. Perfect for beginners and experienced artists alike, this program offers a relaxing and expressive environment to build confidence and create stunning, atmospheric artwork.

programs.name: Workshop: Watercolour - Wet-On-Wet
programs.description: Explore the fluid beauty of watercolour in this hands-on program focused on the wet-on-wet technique! Participants will learn how to paint on damp paper to create soft blends, dreamy textures, and organic shapes that flow effortlessly across the page. Through guided practice and creative exploration, you’ll experiment with layering colours, controlling water, and embracing the natural movement of paint. Perfect for beginners and experienced artists alike, this program offers a relaxing and expressive environment to build confidence and create stunning, atmospheric artwork.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Workshop: Crafty Creations
programs.description: Join us for a summer craft

programs.name: Workshop: Watercolour - Wet-on-Wet
programs.description: Explore the fluid beauty of watercolour in this hands-on program focused on the wet-on-wet technique! Participants will learn how to paint on damp paper to create soft blends, dreamy textures, and organic shapes that flow effortlessly across the page. Through guided practice and creative exploration, you’ll experiment with layering colours, controlling water, and embracing the natural movement of paint. Perfect for beginners and experienced artists alike, this program offers a relaxing and expressive environment to build confidence and create stunning, atmospheric artwork.

programs.name: Workshop: Watercolour - Wet-On-Wet
programs.description: Explore the fluid beauty of watercolour in this hands-on program focused on the wet-on-wet technique! Participants will learn how to paint on damp paper to create soft blends, dreamy textures, and organic shapes that flow effortlessly across the page. Through guided practice and creative exploration, you’ll experiment with layering colours, controlling water, and embracing the natural movement of paint. Perfect for beginners and experienced artists alike, this program offers a relaxing and expressive environment to build confidence and create stunning, atmospheric artwork.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Boot Camp
programs.description: Bringing the principles of high-intensity interval training to the pool, this fast-paced activity uses a variety of exercises and is delivered in both shallow and deep water.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.
programs.location: The Elms Cmty School and Pool

programs.name: Cooking: Cake Decorating
programs.description: Always wanted to learn how to decorate a cake? Come harness your creative skills in a class designed to teach you the basics of frosting and piping. Here you will learn how to level a cake and apply decorative patterns as well as write out names and messages. The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. Notify staff at your location of any food allergies or dietary restrictions you may have.

programs.name: Badminton - Court
programs.description: Hit the court for non-stop badminton play. Whether you are a new or experienced player, want to play often or occasionally, drop-in badminton - court offers something for everyone. Though not formally structured, guidelines are used to ensure participation is balanced. Supervision, use of equipment and capacity limits vary by location, please check ahead. It is free to reserve a court for up to 5 players, fees may apply at the time of check in and are per person. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in. Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment. To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Fusing Glass Workshop: 3 Pendants
programs.description: Create three one-of-a-kind glass pendants. Decorate pieces of glass to make your own beautiful jewellery. Please bring $35 cash to the class for materials. Fusing occurs off-site and pick up of works to be coordinated with the instructor.

programs.name: Pickleball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Etobicoke York Inclusive Request
programs.description: Our inclusive services are designed for participants who may require additional assistance to participate in recreation activities. Inclusive staff assist participants by providing modifications in program instruction, movement, equipment and communication. They also promote and encourage socialization amongst peers. Assignments of Inclusive staff are based on individual application and availability.As this is only a request, participants who waitlist for this activity will be contacted by Adapted and Inclusive Recreation Services for follow-up.
